reasons: Chou can reach Gord's backline position and interrupt his safe casting pattern.
Gord is vulnerable when mobile fighters force him to reposition instead of channeling damage freely.

proof.summary: Gord wants to stand at range and cast, while Chou can dive, displace, and prevent him from safely channeling damage.
worksBestWhen: Chou flanks instead of walking through Gord's frontline.
Chou saves control for Gord's ultimate or escape attempt.
failureCases: Chou engages through multiple control skills before reaching Gord.

reasons: Saber can punish Gord's immobility with targeted burst.
Gord has difficulty surviving if Saber reaches him before Gord controls the approach.

proof.summary: Gord wants to cast safely from distance, while Saber can convert one backline access window into airborne burst.
worksBestWhen: Saber waits in fog or bush.
Gord has already used his stun or control skill.
failureCases: Gord has Purify, stasis timing, or strong peel.
Saber dives into grouped enemies without follow-up.

reasons: Natalia can threaten Gord from unexpected angles and punish him before he sets up long-range damage.
Conceal and silence pressure force Gord to play farther back, reducing his zone control.

proof.summary: Gord relies on safe casting space, while Natalia pressures that space with concealed backline access and silence threat.
worksBestWhen: Natalia attacks after Gord uses his stun.
Natalia has vision control around mid bushes.
failureCases: Gord's team keeps reveal or trap coverage.
Natalia is forced to approach through grouped enemies.

reasons: Lolita can reduce Gord's poke value by blocking or absorbing projectile-style pressure for her team.
This gives her team a safer path to walk through Gord's zoning during siege or objectives.

proof.summary: Gord wants to wear enemies down with ranged skills, while Lolita can protect a team from projectile-based pressure and help them cross space.
worksBestWhen: The team walks behind Lolita's shield during siege or objective fights.
failureCases: Gord angles skills around the shield.
Gord catches Lolita after her shield timing ends.

reasons: Hayabusa can bypass Gord's range by approaching from shadows and threatening single-target burst.
Gord is especially vulnerable when isolated or when his control skill is unavailable.

proof.summary: Gord wants predictable front-to-back fights, while Hayabusa can attack from side angles and punish isolated positioning.
worksBestWhen: Hayabusa has vision of Gord's position before diving.
Gord is separated from tank peel.
failureCases: Gord stays grouped and Hayabusa's ultimate damage gets split.
Hayabusa dives without tracking enemy crowd control.
